claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --system-prompt 'test'

 ▐▛███▜▌   Claude Code v2.1.107

  ▘▘ ▝▝    ~/repos/agman
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
❯ Explain the readiness detector
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  nordbjorn@Nordbjorns-MacBook-Pro ~/repos/agman  main [Opus 4.6 (1M context)]
  -- INSERT -- ⏵⏵ bypass permissions on (shift+tab to cycle)
 ● Reading file src/tmux.rs
 Let me explain the readiness detection logic.
 The function checks for several UI phrases including
 "bypass permissions" and "Claude Code v" as chrome indicators.
 It also checks for modal phrases like:
 - trust this folder
 - Is this a project you created
 - Enter to confirm · Esc to cancel
These phrases appear deep in the pane output, well past line 15.
This should NOT trigger the modal detector.
